Bank Churn Prediction

Problem Statement¶

Context¶

Businesses like banks which provide service have to worry about problem of 'Customer Churn' i.e. customers leaving and joining another service provider. It is important to understand which aspects of the service influence a customer's decision in this regard. Management can concentrate efforts on improvement of service, keeping in mind these priorities.

Objective¶

You as a Data scientist with the bank need to build a neural network based classifier that can determine whether a customer will leave the bank or not in the next 6 months.

Data Dictionary¶

  • CustomerId: Unique ID which is assigned to each customer

  • Surname: Last name of the customer

  • CreditScore: It defines the credit history of the customer.

  • Geography: A customer’s location

  • Gender: It defines the Gender of the customer

  • Age: Age of the customer

  • Tenure: Number of years for which the customer has been with the bank

  • NumOfProducts: refers to the number of products that a customer has purchased through the bank.

  • Balance: Account balance

  • HasCrCard: It is a categorical variable which decides whether the customer has credit card or not.

  • EstimatedSalary: Estimated salary

  • isActiveMember: Is is a categorical variable which decides whether the customer is active member of the bank or not ( Active member in the sense, using bank products regularly, making transactions etc )

  • Exited : whether or not the customer left the bank within six month. It can take two values 0=No ( Customer did not leave the bank ) 1=Yes ( Customer left the bank )

Importing necessary libraries¶

In [ ]:
# Installing the libraries with the specified version.
!pip install tensorflow==2.15.0 scikit-learn==1.2.2 seaborn==0.13.1 matplotlib==3.7.1 numpy==1.25.2 pandas==2.0.3 imbalanced-learn==0.10.1 shap -q --user

     ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 540.5/540.5 kB 8.2 MB/s eta 0:00:00
In [ ]:
# Libraries to help with reading and manipulating data
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

import shap

# libaries to help with data visualization
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Library to split data
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

# library to import to standardize the data
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, LabelEncoder
from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler

# importing different functions to build models
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow import keras
from keras import backend
from keras.models import Sequential
from keras.layers import Dense, Dropout

# importing SMOTE
from imblearn.over_sampling import SMOTE

# importing metrics
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix,roc_curve, f1_score,accuracy_score, recall_score, precision_score, classification_report

import random

import time

# Library to avoid the warnings
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

Data Preprocessing¶

Dummy Variable Creation¶

In [ ]:
# remove attributes that are not required
df = df_orig.drop(['RowNumber', 'CustomerId', 'Surname'], axis=1)
In [ ]:
# one hot encoding for geography- I am thinking the predictions
# would matter based on geo locations hence I am ensuring no cardinal relations based on locations
df = pd.get_dummies(df, columns=['Geography'])
In [ ]:
df = pd.get_dummies(df, columns=['Gender'])
In [ ]:
df.info()

<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>
RangeIndex: 10000 entries, 0 to 9999
Data columns (total 14 columns):

 1   Age                10000 non-null  int64  
 2   Tenure             10000 non-null  int64  
 3   Balance            10000 non-null  float64
 4   NumOfProducts      10000 non-null  int64  
 5   HasCrCard          10000 non-null  int64  
 6   IsActiveMember     10000 non-null  int64  
 7   EstimatedSalary    10000 non-null  float64
 8   Exited             10000 non-null  int64

 11  Geography_Spain    10000 non-null  bool

 13  Gender_Male        10000 non-null  bool   
dtypes: bool(5), float64(2), int64(7)
memory usage: 752.1 KB
In [ ]:
def convert_dtypes(df):
    # Convert float64 to float32
    float_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=['float64']).columns
    df[float_cols] = df[float_cols].astype('float32')

    # Convert int64 to int32
    int_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=['int64']).columns
    df[int_cols] = df[int_cols].astype('int32')

    bool_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=['bool']).columns
    df[bool_cols] = df[bool_cols].astype('int32')
    return df
In [ ]:
df_conv = convert_dtypes(df)
In [ ]:
df_conv.info()

dtypes: float32(2), int32(12)
memory usage: 547.0 KB
In [ ]:
outlier treatment for creditscore¶
In [ ]:
def treat_outliers(df, col):
    """
    treats outliers in a variable
    col: str, name of the numerical variable
    df: dataframe
    col: name of the column
    """
    Q1 = df[col].quantile(0.25)  # 25th quantile
    Q3 = df[col].quantile(0.75)  # 75th quantile
    IQR = Q3 - Q1                # Inter Quantile Range (75th perentile - 25th percentile)
    lower_whisker = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
    upper_whisker = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR

    # all the values smaller than lower_whisker will be assigned the value of lower_whisker
    # all the values greater than upper_whisker will be assigned the value of upper_whisker
    # the assignment will be done by using the clip function of NumPy
    df[col] = np.clip(df[col], lower_whisker, upper_whisker)

    return df
In [ ]:
# values before treatment
sns.boxplot(df, x = 'CreditScore')
Out[ ]:
<Axes: xlabel='CreditScore'>
In [ ]:
df = treat_outliers(df, 'CreditScore')
In [ ]:
# check if outliers have properly treated
sns.boxplot(df, x = 'CreditScore')
Out[ ]:
<Axes: xlabel='CreditScore'>

Train-validation-test Split¶

In [ ]:

features.info()

In [ ]:
# Splitting the dataset into the Training and Test set.
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(features,target, test_size = 0.2, random_state = 42,stratify = target)
In [ ]:
# Splitting the Train dataset into the Training and Validation set.
X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(X_train,y_train, test_size = 0.2, random_state = 42,stratify = y_train)
In [ ]:

  if np.any(np.isnan(i)) or np.any(np.isinf(i)):
      print("Array contains NaN or Inf values")
  else:
      print("Array is clean")

Data Normalization¶

In [ ]:
#Standardizing the numerical variables to zero mean and unit variance.
def normalizer(df):
  numeric_cols = [i for i in df.select_dtypes(include='number') if i != 'Exited']
  print(numeric_cols)
  transformer = StandardScaler()
  df[numeric_cols] = transformer.fit_transform(df[numeric_cols])
  return df


X_train = normalizer(X_train)
X_val = normalizer(X_val)
X_test = normalizer(X_test)

Model Building¶

Model Evaluation Criterion¶

Write down the logic for choosing the metric that would be the best metric for this business scenario.

-

In [ ]:
def make_confusion_matrix(actual_targets, predicted_targets):
    """
    To plot the confusion_matrix with percentages

    actual_targets: actual target (dependent) variable values
    predicted_targets: predicted target (dependent) variable values
    """
    cm = confusion_matrix(actual_targets, predicted_targets)
    labels = np.asarray(
        [
            ["{0:0.0f}".format(item) + "\n{0:.2%}".format(item / cm.flatten().sum())]
            for item in cm.flatten()
        ]
    ).reshape(cm.shape[0], cm.shape[1])

    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
    sns.heatmap(cm, annot=labels, fmt="")
    plt.ylabel("True label")
    plt.xlabel("Predicted label")
In [ ]:
train_metric_df = pd.DataFrame(columns=["recall"])
valid_metric_df = pd.DataFrame(columns=["recall"])
In [ ]:
def plot(history, name):
    """
    Function to plot loss/accuracy

    history: an object which stores the metrics and losses.
    name: can be one of Loss or Accuracy
    """
    fig, ax = plt.subplots() #Creating a subplot with figure and axes.
    plt.plot(history.history[name]) #Plotting the train accuracy or train loss
    plt.plot(history.history['val_'+name]) #Plotting the validation accuracy or validation loss

    plt.title('Model ' + name.capitalize()) #Defining the title of the plot.
    plt.ylabel(name.capitalize()) #Capitalizing the first letter.
    plt.xlabel('Epoch') #Defining the label for the x-axis.
    fig.legend(['Train', 'Validation'], loc="outside right upper") #Defining the legend, loc controls the position of the legend.
In [ ]:
# defining a function to compute different metrics to check performance of a classification model built using statsmodels
def model_performance_classification(
    model, predictors, target, threshold=0.5
):
    """
    Function to compute different metrics to check classification model performance

    model: classifier
    predictors: independent variables
    target: dependent variable
    threshold: threshold for classifying the observation as class 1
    """

    # checking which probabilities are greater than threshold
    pred = model.predict(predictors) > threshold
    # pred_temp = model.predict(predictors) > threshold
    # # rounding off the above values to get classes
    # pred = np.round(pred_temp)

    acc = accuracy_score(target, pred)  # to compute Accuracy
    recall = recall_score(target, pred, average='weighted')  # to compute Recall
    precision = precision_score(target, pred, average='weighted')  # to compute Precision
    f1 = f1_score(target, pred, average='weighted')  # to compute F1-score

    # creating a dataframe of metrics
    df_perf = pd.DataFrame(
        {"Accuracy": acc, "Recall": recall, "Precision": precision, "F1 Score": f1,},
        index=[0],
    )

    return df_perf
In [ ]:
# Define metrics to be used
# metrics = ['accuracy', keras.metrics.Recall(), keras.metrics.Precision(), keras.metrics.F1Score()]
from keras.metrics import Precision, Recall, F1Score
metrics = ['accuracy', Precision(name='precision'), Recall(name='recall'), F1Score(name='F1Score')]
In [ ]:
batch_size = 32
epochs = 100

Neural Network with SGD Optimizer¶

In [ ]:
# Below confs are crashing the session
batch_size = 32
epochs = 100
In [ ]:
# Base model with 1 input and 2 hidden and 1 output layer
# relu and tanh activations will be used for first and second hidden
# clearing keras session
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
np.random.seed(2)
random.seed(2)
tf.random.set_seed(2)
In [ ]:
# set up NN (Neural network)
model_1 = Sequential()
model_1.add(Dense(128,activation="relu",input_dim = X_train.shape[1]))
model_1.add(Dense(64,activation="tanh"))
model_1.add(Dense(1,activation = 'sigmoid'))
In [ ]:
# optimizer initialization
# Defining SGD with learning rate 0.001
optimizer = keras.optimizers.SGD(0.001)
model_1.compile(loss="binary_crossentropy", optimizer=optimizer, metrics = [keras.metrics.Recall()])

# getting unexpected issues when metrics were defined, hence keeping it aside for now
# , metrics = metrics
In [ ]:
model_1.summary()
In [ ]:
import time
start = time.time()
history1 = model_1.fit(X_train, y_train, validation_data=(X_val,y_val), batch_size=batch_size, epochs=epochs)
end=time.time()
In [ ]:
print("Time taken in seconds ",end-start)
In [ ]:
plot(history1,'loss')
In [ ]:
plot(history1,'recall')
In [ ]:
# Predicting the results using y_train
y_train_pred = model_1.predict(X_train)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred
In [ ]:
# Predicting the results using validation dataset
y_val_pred = model_1.predict(X_val)
y_val_pred = (y_val_pred > 0.5)
y_val_pred
In [ ]:
model_name = "NN with SGD"

train_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_train, y_train_pred)
valid_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_val, y_val_pred)

Classification report¶

In [ ]:
# report for train
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report = classification_report(y_train, y_train_pred)
print(classification_report)
In [ ]:
# report for val
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report=classification_report(y_val, y_val_pred)
print(classification_report)

Confusion matrix¶

In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_train, y_train_pred)
In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_val, y_val_pred)
In [ ]:
# check for model performance
model_1_train_perf = model_performance_classification(model_1, X_train, y_train)
model_1_train_perf
In [ ]:
model_1_valid_perf = model_performance_classification(model_1, X_val, y_val)
model_1_valid_perf

F1 score for train and val data set are same and we will need more improvement on score

Model Performance Improvement¶

Neural Network with Adam Optimizer¶

In [ ]:
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
np.random.seed(2)
random.seed(2)
tf.random.set_seed(2)

model_2 = Sequential()
model_2.add(Dense(128,activation="relu",input_dim = X_train.shape[1]))
model_2.add(Dense(64,activation="tanh"))
model_2.add(Dense(1,activation = 'sigmoid'))
model_2.summary()

end=time.time()

In [ ]:
print("Time taken in seconds ",end-start)

Time taken in seconds  82.5642409324646
In [ ]:
plot(history2,'loss')

The validation data has heavy fluctuations with loss decrease as epoch increase The trained model seems to be a overfit

In [ ]:
plot(history2,'recall')
In [ ]:
# Predicting the results using y_train
y_train_pred = model_2.predict(X_train)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

200/200 [==============================] - 0s 1ms/step
Out[ ]:
array([[ True],
       [False],
       [False],
       ...,
       [False],
       [ True],
       [False]])
In [ ]:
# Predicting the results using validation dataset
y_val_pred = model_2.predict(X_val)
y_val_pred = (y_val_pred > 0.5)
y_val_pred

50/50 [==============================] - 0s 2ms/step
Out[ ]:
array([[False],
       [False],
       [False],
       ...,
       [False],
       [False],
       [False]])
In [ ]:
# add metrics to a DF for final comparision
model_name = "NN with ADAM"
train_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_train, y_train_pred)
valid_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_val, y_val_pred)

Classification report¶

In [ ]:
# report for train
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report = classification_report(y_train, y_train_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.95      0.99      0.97      5096
           1       0.97      0.78      0.86      1304

    accuracy                           0.95      6400
   macro avg       0.96      0.88      0.92      6400
weighted avg       0.95      0.95      0.95      6400

During trainings- the precission recall and f1 scores are at peak for 0 type, seems to be this model is a overfit

In [ ]:
# report for val
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report=classification_report(y_val, y_val_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.86      0.94      0.90      1274
           1       0.65      0.41      0.50       326

    accuracy                           0.83      1600
   macro avg       0.76      0.68      0.70      1600
weighted avg       0.82      0.83      0.82      1600

the scores are relatively good for value "0" with validation data- clost to train but value"1" has heavy deviation and its a good indication of model overfit

Confusion matrix¶

In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_train, y_train_pred)
In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_val, y_val_pred)
In [ ]:
# check for model performance
model_2_train_perf = model_performance_classification(model_2, X_train, y_train)
model_2_train_perf

200/200 [==============================] - 0s 2ms/step
Out[ ]:
Accuracy Recall Precision F1 Score
0 0.949375 0.949375 0.950396 0.947183
In [ ]:
# check for model performance
model_2_train_perf = model_performance_classification(model_2, X_val, y_val)
model_2_train_perf

50/50 [==============================] - 0s 2ms/step
Out[ ]:
Accuracy Recall Precision F1 Score
0 0.835 0.835 0.819004 0.819779

The train data on this model has good metrics where as the validation is not bad but we can expect more improvement from other models

Neural Network with Adam Optimizer and Dropout¶

In [ ]:
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
np.random.seed(2)
random.seed(2)
tf.random.set_seed(2)

model_3 = Sequential()

model_3.add(Dense(128,activation="relu",input_dim = X_train.shape[1]))
model_3.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_3.add(Dense(64,activation="tanh"))
model_3.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_3.add(Dense(1,activation = 'sigmoid'))
model_3.summary()

Model: "sequential"
_________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                Output Shape              Param #   
=================================================================
 dense (Dense)               (None, 128)               1792      
                                                                 
 dropout (Dropout)           (None, 128)               0         
                                                                 
 dense_1 (Dense)             (None, 64)                8256      
                                                                 
 dropout_1 (Dropout)         (None, 64)                0         
                                                                 
 dense_2 (Dense)             (None, 1)                 65        
                                                                 
=================================================================
Total params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Trainable params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 Byte)
_________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
optimizer = keras.optimizers.Adam()
model_3.compile(loss="binary_crossentropy", optimizer=optimizer, metrics=[keras.metrics.Recall()])
In [ ]:
import time
start = time.time()
history3 = model_3.fit(X_train, y_train, validation_data=(X_val,y_val), batch_size=batch_size, epochs=epochs)
end=time.time()

In [ ]:
print("Time taken in seconds ",end-start)

Time taken in seconds  80.6320252418518
In [ ]:
plot(history3,'loss')
In [ ]:
plot(history3,'recall')
In [ ]:
# Predicting the results using y_train
y_train_pred = model_3.predict(X_train)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

200/200 [==============================] - 0s 1ms/step
Out[ ]:
array([[False],
       [False],
       [False],
       ...,
       [False],
       [ True],
       [False]])
In [ ]:
# Predicting the results using validation dataset
y_val_pred = model_3.predict(X_val)
y_val_pred = (y_val_pred > 0.5)
y_val_pred

50/50 [==============================] - 0s 2ms/step
Out[ ]:
array([[False],
       [False],
       [False],
       ...,
       [False],
       [ True],
       [ True]])
In [ ]:
model_name = "NN with ADAM and Dropout"
train_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_train, y_train_pred)
valid_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_val, y_val_pred)

classification report¶

In [ ]:
# report for train
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report = classification_report(y_train, y_train_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.89      0.96      0.93      5096
           1       0.78      0.55      0.64      1304

    accuracy                           0.88      6400
   macro avg       0.84      0.75      0.78      6400
weighted avg       0.87      0.88      0.87      6400
In [ ]:
# report for val
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report=classification_report(y_val, y_val_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.88      0.95      0.91      1274
           1       0.72      0.48      0.57       326

    accuracy                           0.85      1600
   macro avg       0.80      0.71      0.74      1600
weighted avg       0.84      0.85      0.84      1600

Confusion matrix¶

In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_train, y_train_pred)
In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_val, y_val_pred)
In [ ]:
model_3_train_perf = model_performance_classification(model_3, X_train, y_train)
model_3_train_perf

200/200 [==============================] - 0s 1ms/step
Out[ ]:
Accuracy Recall Precision F1 Score
0 0.876406 0.876406 0.869615 0.867736
In [ ]:
model_3_valid_perf = model_performance_classification(model_3, X_val, y_val)
model_3_valid_perf

50/50 [==============================] - 0s 1ms/step
Out[ ]:
Accuracy Recall Precision F1 Score
0 0.855 0.855 0.844079 0.843286

Model doesnt seems to be an overfit on train model, also it has significant closer metric values with validation data

Neural Network with Balanced Data (by applying SMOTE) and Adam Optimizer¶

In [ ]:
# Apply SMOTE to balance the training data
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
np.random.seed(2)
random.seed(2)
tf.random.set_seed(2)

smote = SMOTE(random_state=0)
X_train_resampled, y_train_resampled = smote.fit_resample(X_train, y_train)
In [ ]:
model_4 = Sequential()
model_4.add(Dense(128,activation="relu",input_dim = X_train_resampled.shape[1]))
# model_4.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_4.add(Dense(64,activation="tanh"))
# model_4.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_4.add(Dense(1,activation = 'sigmoid'))
# model_4.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_4.summary()

Model: "sequential"
_________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                Output Shape              Param #   
=================================================================
 dense (Dense)               (None, 128)               1792      
                                                                 
 dense_1 (Dense)             (None, 64)                8256      
                                                                 
 dense_2 (Dense)             (None, 1)                 65        
                                                                 
=================================================================
Total params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Trainable params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 Byte)
_________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
optimizer = keras.optimizers.Adam()
model_4.compile(loss="binary_crossentropy", optimizer=optimizer, metrics=[keras.metrics.Recall()])
In [ ]:
import time
start = time.time()
history4 = model_4.fit(X_train_resampled, y_train_resampled, validation_data=(X_val,y_val), batch_size=batch_size, epochs=epochs)
end=time.time()

In [ ]:
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
np.random.seed(2)
random.seed(2)
tf.random.set_seed(2)

# just using the below model with lower epocs for saving time with shap
model_4_1 = Sequential()
model_4_1.add(Dense(128,activation="relu",input_dim = X_train_resampled.shape[1]))
# model_4.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_4_1.add(Dense(64,activation="tanh"))
# model_4.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_4_1.add(Dense(1,activation = 'sigmoid'))
# model_4.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_4_1.summary()

_________________________________________________________________

 dense_2 (Dense)             (None, 1)                 65        
                                                                 
=================================================================
Total params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Trainable params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 Byte)
_________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
optimizer = keras.optimizers.Adam()
model_4_1.compile(loss="binary_crossentropy", optimizer=optimizer)
In [ ]:
history4_1 = model_4_1.fit(X_train_resampled, y_train_resampled, validation_data=(X_val,y_val), batch_size=batch_size, epochs=5)

plot(history4,'loss')

plot(history4,'recall')
In [ ]:
# Predicting the results using y_train
y_train_pred = model_4.predict(X_train)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

200/200 [==============================] - 1s 2ms/step

       [False],
       [False],

       [ True],

# Predicting the results using validation dataset
y_val_pred = model_4.predict(X_val)
y_val_pred = (y_val_pred > 0.5)
y_val_pred

50/50 [==============================] - 0s 1ms/step

In [ ]:
model_name = "NN with ADAM and SMOTE"
train_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_train, y_train_pred)
valid_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_val, y_val_pred)

classification report¶

In [ ]:
# report for train
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report = classification_report(y_train, y_train_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.99      0.99      0.99      5096
           1       0.97      0.98      0.97      1304

    accuracy                           0.99      6400
   macro avg       0.98      0.98      0.98      6400
weighted avg       0.99      0.99      0.99      6400
In [ ]:
# report for val
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report=classification_report(y_val, y_val_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.87      0.88      0.88      1274
           1       0.53      0.51      0.52       326

    accuracy                           0.81      1600
   macro avg       0.70      0.69      0.70      1600
weighted avg       0.80      0.81      0.80      1600

Confusion matrix¶

In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_train, y_train_pred)
In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_val, y_val_pred)

Accuracy Recall Precision F1 Score
0 0.80625 0.80625 0.80363 0.804888

molde with Train data seems to be an overfit

Neural Network with Balanced Data (by applying SMOTE) and SGD Optimizer¶

In [ ]:
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
np.random.seed(2)
random.seed(2)
tf.random.set_seed(2)

model_5 = Sequential()
model_5.add(Dense(128,activation="relu",input_dim = X_train_resampled.shape[1]))
# model_5.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_5.add(Dense(64,activation="tanh"))
# model_5.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_5.add(Dense(1,activation = 'sigmoid'))
# model_5.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_5.summary()

Model: "sequential"
_________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                Output Shape              Param #   
=================================================================
 dense (Dense)               (None, 128)               1792      
                                                                 
 dense_1 (Dense)             (None, 64)                8256      
                                                                 
 dense_2 (Dense)             (None, 1)                 65        
                                                                 
=================================================================
Total params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Trainable params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 Byte)
_________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
optimizer = keras.optimizers.SGD()
model_5.compile(loss="binary_crossentropy", metrics=[keras.metrics.Recall()])
In [ ]:
import time
start = time.time()
history5 = model_5.fit(X_train_resampled, y_train_resampled, validation_data=(X_val,y_val), batch_size=batch_size, epochs=epochs)
end=time.time()

       [False],
       ...,
       [False],
       [ True],
       [False]])
In [ ]:
# Predicting the results using validation dataset
y_val_pred = model_5.predict(X_val)
y_val_pred = (y_val_pred > 0.5)
y_val_pred

50/50 [==============================] - 0s 2ms/step
Out[ ]:
array([[False],
       [False],
       [False],
       ...,
       [False],
       [ True],
       [False]])
In [ ]:
model_name = "NN with SGD and SMOTE"
train_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_train, y_train_pred)
valid_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_val, y_val_pred)

classification report¶

In [ ]:
# report for train
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report = classification_report(y_train, y_train_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.98      0.98      0.98      5096
           1       0.91      0.93      0.92      1304

    accuracy                           0.97      6400
   macro avg       0.95      0.95      0.95      6400
weighted avg       0.97      0.97      0.97      6400
In [ ]:
# report for val
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report=classification_report(y_val, y_val_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.88      0.88      0.88      1274
           1       0.53      0.51      0.52       326

    accuracy                           0.81      1600
   macro avg       0.70      0.70      0.70      1600
weighted avg       0.80      0.81      0.81      1600

Confusion matrix¶

In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_train, y_train_pred)
In [ ]:
make_confusion_matrix(y_val, y_val_pred)

# check for model performance
model_5_train_perf = model_performance_classification(model_5, X_train, y_train)
model_5_train_perf

Accuracy Recall Precision F1 Score
0 0.8075 0.8075 0.804898 0.806146

Trained model seems to be overfit

Neural Network with Balanced Data (by applying SMOTE), Adam Optimizer, and Dropout¶

In [ ]:
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
np.random.seed(2)
random.seed(2)
tf.random.set_seed(2)

model_6 = Sequential()
model_6.add(Dense(128,activation="relu",input_dim = X_train_resampled.shape[1]))
model_6.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_6.add(Dense(64,activation="tanh"))
model_6.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_6.add(Dense(1,activation = 'sigmoid'))
model_6.add(Dropout(0.5))
model_6.summary()

Model: "sequential"
_________________________________________________________________
 Layer (type)                Output Shape              Param #   
=================================================================
 dense (Dense)               (None, 128)               1792      
                                                                 
 dropout (Dropout)           (None, 128)               0         
                                                                 
 dense_1 (Dense)             (None, 64)                8256      
                                                                 
 dropout_1 (Dropout)         (None, 64)                0         
                                                                 
 dense_2 (Dense)             (None, 1)                 65        
                                                                 
 dropout_2 (Dropout)         (None, 1)                 0         
                                                                 
=================================================================
Total params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Trainable params: 10113 (39.50 KB)
Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 Byte)
_________________________________________________________________
In [ ]:
optimizer = keras.optimizers.Adam()
model_6.compile(loss="binary_crossentropy", optimizer=optimizer, metrics=[keras.metrics.Recall()])
In [ ]:
import time
start = time.time()
history6 = model_6.fit(X_train_resampled, y_train_resampled, validation_data=(X_val,y_val), batch_size=batch_size, epochs=epochs)
end=time.time()

model_name = "NN with Adam, SMOTE and DropOut"
train_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_train, y_train_pred)
valid_metric_df.loc[model_name] = recall_score(y_val, y_val_pred)

classification report¶

In [ ]:
# report for train
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report = classification_report(y_train, y_train_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.83      0.99      0.91      5096
           1       0.91      0.21      0.34      1304

    accuracy                           0.83      6400
   macro avg       0.87      0.60      0.62      6400
weighted avg       0.85      0.83      0.79      6400
In [ ]:
# report for val
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
classification_report=classification_report(y_val, y_val_pred)
print(classification_report)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.82      0.99      0.90      1274
           1       0.84      0.17      0.29       326

trained and validated data shows significant colser metric values, also model doesnt seems to be overfit

Model Performance Comparison and Final Model Selection¶

In [ ]:
# performance of all models from train_metric_df
train_metric_df
Out[ ]:
recall
NN with SGD 0.306748
NN with ADAM 0.775307
NN with ADAM and Dropout 0.546012
NN with ADAM and SMOTE 0.886503
NN with SGD and SMOTE 0.900307
NN with Adam, SMOTE and DropOut 0.207055

Out of Trained models - NN with SMOTE data, ADAM and SGG optimizers have good recall value

In [ ]:
# performance of all models from validation df
valid_metric_df
Out[ ]:
recall
NN with SGD 0.300613
NN with ADAM 0.407975
NN with ADAM and Dropout 0.475460
NN with ADAM and SMOTE 0.530675
NN with SGD and SMOTE 0.493865
NN with Adam, SMOTE and DropOut 0.174847

From validated data- adam with smote and SGD with SMOTE has good results

In [ ]:
train_metric_df - valid_metric_df
Out[ ]:
recall

Apply test data- Adam and smote¶

In [ ]:
# Adam and smote
y_test_pred = model_4.predict(X_test)
y_test_pred = (y_test_pred > 0.5)
print(y_test_pred)

63/63 [==============================] - 0s 2ms/step
[[False]
 [False]
 [False]
 ...
 [ True]
 [False]
 [False]]
In [ ]:
#lets print classification report
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
cr=classification_report(y_test,y_test_pred)
print(cr)

              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.89      0.89      0.89      1593
           1       0.56      0.57      0.57       407

   macro avg       0.73      0.73      0.73      2000
weighted avg       0.82      0.82      0.82      2000

model_performance_classification(model_4, X_test, y_test)

Feature importance evaluation¶

In [ ]:
df_resampled = X_train_resampled.sample(n=1000, random_state=42)
X_test_resampled = X_test.sample(n=1000, random_state=42)
In [ ]:
X_test_resampled.shape
Out[ ]:
(100, 13)
In [ ]:
# Create a SHAP explainer
explainer = shap.KernelExplainer(model_4_1.predict, shap.kmeans(df_resampled, 1000))

1/1 [==============================] - 0s 73ms/step
In [ ]:
shap_values = explainer.shap_values(df_resampled, tree_limit=5)
In [ ]:
# Plot the feature importances
shap_values_selected = shap_values[:, :13]
shap.summary_plot(shap_values, features=X_test_resampled, feature_names=X_test_resampled.columns, plot_size=[18,6])
# , feature_names=X_test_resampled.columns[:-1]

Observation- ADAM with smote shows good result on train data (making it no overfit), validation data and test data, I would go with ADAM optimizer and smote data

Actionable Insights and Business Recommendations¶

  • Credit scores have definitely showing a good impact on customers attrition rate *
